articles.title: Sinner Wins Rome to Complete Full Set of All Nine ATP Masters Titles
articles.summary: Jannik Sinner beat Casper Ruud 6-4 6-4 at the Italian Open on May 18 to become the first player to complete the career Golden Masters, winning all nine ATP Masters 1000 titles. The Golden Masters is the tennis equivalent of collecting every major trophy at the sport's second-highest level, a feat no one had ever managed before. Sinner did it on home soil in Rome, making the moment even more special.
articles.content:
Jannik Sinner just made history in Rome. The world number one beat Norway's Casper Ruud 6-4 6-4 in the Italian Open final today to complete the career Golden Masters, meaning he has now won all nine ATP Masters 1000 titles. Nobody had ever done it before.

The ATP Masters 1000 tournaments are the biggest events in tennis outside of the four Grand Slams. There are nine of them spread across the season, played in cities including Paris, Miami, Madrid, and Monte Carlo. Winning all nine is an extraordinary achievement because the fields are full of the world's best players and each tournament is played on a different surface or in different conditions. Sinner has now conquered every single one.

What makes this moment even more cinematic is where it happened. Sinner is Italian, and the Italian Open is played in Rome. Completing the Golden Masters in front of a home crowd, on the clay courts of the Foro Italico, is the kind of storybook ending that sport very rarely delivers. The crowd would have been absolutely electric.

Ruud, the world number two from Norway, is no easy opponent. He is a clay-court specialist who has made multiple Grand Slam finals. The fact that Sinner dispatched him in straight sets, 6-4 6-4, without even needing a third, shows just how dominant Sinner is right now. This is a player operating at a level that is simply above everyone else in the game.
keyPoints: Jannik Sinner beat Casper Ruud 6-4 6-4 to win the Italian Open on May 18
The win completed the career Golden Masters, making Sinner the first player to win all nine ATP Masters 1000 titles
The Golden Masters is tennis's most complete achievement at the Masters level, below only the Grand Slams
Sinner is world number one and completed the feat on home soil in Rome
The straight-sets victory underlines how dominant Sinner currently is on the ATP Tour

articles.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda a Global Emergency
articles.summary: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday, 17 May 2026. The outbreak is caused by the rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, which has no approved vaccine or treatment. Over 336 suspected cases and 88 deaths have already been reported, and the virus has crossed the border from DRC into Uganda's capital, Kampala.
articles.content:
This is serious. The World Health Organization officially declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda a "public health emergency of international concern" on Sunday 17 May. That is the WHO's second-highest alert level under international health rules, and it signals that the rest of the world needs to pay attention.

The outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, a rare and dangerous variant. As of Sunday, there were 10 confirmed cases and 336 suspected cases in the DRC, with 88 reported deaths. Two confirmed cases have also been detected in Uganda's capital, Kampala, both linked to travellers arriving from the DRC. One of those individuals has already died.

What makes this especially alarming is that no approved vaccine or specific treatment exists for the Bundibugyo strain. The virus was first confirmed on 15 May after lab tests on samples from Ituri Province in eastern DRC, but the first known suspected case dates back to 24 April. That three-week gap between the first suspected case and official confirmation has raised serious concerns about how quickly health systems can catch an outbreak like this.

The WHO also made an unusual move in how it issued this declaration. Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus declared the emergency without first consulting an expert emergency committee, which is the normal process. It was the first time that has ever happened under WHO rules. Ghebreyesus was clear that the outbreak does not yet meet the criteria for a full pandemic emergency, but warned that neighbouring countries are at high risk of further spread. Conflict in Ituri Province, weak healthcare infrastructure, and high population movement across the region are all making containment harder.
keyPoints: WHO declared the DRC and Uganda Ebola outbreak a global health emergency on 17 May 2026
The outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which there is no approved vaccine or treatment
Over 336 suspected cases and 88 deaths have been reported, with cases spreading into Uganda's capital Kampala
The WHO Director-General issued this declaration without first consulting an expert committee, a first in WHO history
The outbreak does not yet meet pandemic criteria, but neighbouring countries have been warned to prepare

articles.title: Demna Shuts Down Times Square for Gucci's GucciCore Cruise 2027
articles.summary: Gucci creative director Demna staged his debut Cruise collection, titled GucciCore, directly on the streets of Times Square in New York on May 16, shutting down blocks of Midtown Manhattan for a fashion show unlike anything the city has seen. The show marks the fourth chapter of Demna's evolving creative vision at Gucci, following La Famiglia, Generation Gucci, and Primavera, and introduces a permanent core wardrobe designed to last beyond any single season. Celebrity walkers including Cindy Crawford, Paris Hilton, and Tom Brady shared the runway with professional models as Mariah Carey, Kim Kardashian, and Lindsay Lohan watched from the front row.
articles.content:
Fashion just took over one of the most chaotic intersections on earth, and it looked incredible. Gucci creative director Demna unveiled his first Cruise collection for the house in the middle of Midtown Manhattan on Saturday night, shutting down multiple blocks of Times Square and commandeering every towering billboard in sight. This is the kind of fashion moment people talk about for years.

The reason for the location is simple: New York has held a singular place in Gucci's history for over 70 years, with the house opening its first store outside of Italy in the city in 1953. The choice of location was deeply referential, nodding to the Gucci Galleria, an exclusive salon hidden above the Fifth Avenue flagship during the 1980s, where select clients accessed the space. Demna took all of that history and blew it up onto the biggest screens in the world.

Demna described GucciCore as the fourth act of his character studies approach, bringing together the aesthetic languages of La Famiglia, Generation Gucci, and Primavera into one cohesive vision, built around a core wardrobe of staple pieces: the perfect peacoat, the classic trench, the business suit, the essential shirt, and the ultimate pencil skirt alongside elements of Italian glamour. Most of what walked in the show is part of GucciCore, a permanent collection that will evolve over time, shaping his vision by building the foundation of a Gucci wardrobe grounded in pragmatic, wearable pieces that are unmistakably Gucci. That word — permanent — is the real news here. This is not a one-season collection. It is Demna building the DNA of what Gucci will be for the next decade.

The Gucci takeover kicked off with an 8:30 p.m. pre-show video montage that Demna described as contrasting the venue's consumerism and information saturation against the beauty of the world, projected across the rolling digital billboards. Demna went all in on ideas spanning from Gucci Acqua, Gucci Gym, and Palazzo Gucci Hotel to Gucci Pets and even Gucci Life longevity supplements. It was absurd and brilliant in equal measure.

His megawatt cast amplified Demna's vision of being not only about how the clothes are styled, but who wears them — Tom Brady strutting the runway in head-to-toe leather, Alex Consani in a vampy sheer caftan, a brown-haired Paris Hilton in a bright yellow print dress, and Cindy Crawford shutting down the show in a black feathered gown. The front row included Mariah Carey, Lindsay Lohan, and Iman. Signature Gucci motifs were reinterpreted throughout: the iconic Web stripe became a bandeau top, while the Horsebit appeared as stirrup detailing on sharply heeled boots. GucciCore is the rare show that is equally about clothes and culture, and Demna has made it clear that he is just getting started.
keyPoints: Demna staged Gucci's Cruise 2027 show, titled GucciCore, by shutting down multiple blocks of Times Square on May 16
GucciCore is the fourth chapter in Demna's character studies approach at Gucci, following La Famiglia, Generation Gucci, and Primavera
The collection introduces a permanent core wardrobe built on staples like the peacoat, trench coat, and pencil skirt rather than seasonal trends
Celebrity walkers included Tom Brady, Paris Hilton, and Cindy Crawford, with Mariah Carey, Kim Kardashian, and Lindsay Lohan in the front row
The show honored Gucci's New York roots, 73 years after the house opened its first store outside Italy on Fifth Avenue in 1953

articles.title: Mourinho Agrees Two-Year Deal to Return as Real Madrid Coach
articles.summary: Jose Mourinho is heading back to Real Madrid, agreeing a two-year deal to take charge of one of the biggest clubs in world football. It is a stunning return for the Portuguese manager, who previously led Real Madrid between 2010 and 2013. This is one of the most talked-about managerial appointments in years.
articles.content:
Jose Mourinho is going back to the Bernabeu. The 63-year-old has agreed a two-year deal to return as Real Madrid head coach, in what is a massive moment for club football. It is a reunion that fans have debated for years, and now it is officially happening.

Mourinho first managed Real Madrid from 2010 to 2013, winning La Liga in the 2011/12 season in record-breaking fashion. His time there was electric but turbulent, marked by fierce rivalry with Pep Guardiola's Barcelona and reportedly difficult relationships with some players. He left under a cloud, but his record on the pitch was hard to argue with.

Since then, Mourinho has had spells at Chelsea, Manchester United, Tottenham Hotspur, AS Roma, and Fenerbahce, with mixed results. His return to Real Madrid signals that the club believes his experience and winning mentality are exactly what they need right now. Two years gives him time to build something serious.

For Real Madrid fans, this is a big swing. Mourinho knows the club, knows the pressure, and knows what it takes to win at the very top. Whether the second chapter can match or even top the first is the question everyone in football will be asking.
keyPoints: Jose Mourinho has agreed a two-year deal to return as Real Madrid head coach
This is his second stint at the club, having previously managed Real Madrid from 2010 to 2013
He won La Liga with Real Madrid during his first spell there
Mourinho has also managed Chelsea, Manchester United, Tottenham, Roma, and Fenerbahce since leaving
The appointment is one of the most high-profile managerial moves in recent football history

articles.title: Swatch Royal Pop Sparks Global Riots, 30+ Stores Forced to Close
articles.summary: The launch of the Swatch x Audemars Piguet Royal Pop pocket watch on Saturday 16 May descended into worldwide chaos, with brawls, pepper spray, and police dogs deployed from Paris to New York. More than 30 Swatch stores across the UK, US, Europe, and the Middle East were forced to shut their doors after crowds became dangerously large. The Royal Pop, priced at £335, is a collaboration between Swatch and luxury watchmaker Audemars Piguet — and some buyers are already flipping them online for up to £12,500.
articles.content:
A £335 pocket watch just broke the internet and several shopping centres along with it. The Swatch x Audemars Piguet Royal Pop collection launched on Saturday 16 May, and what was supposed to be an exciting product drop turned into a full-blown public safety emergency across multiple continents.

In Paris, police fired tear gas outside a Swatch store. In Milan, a fight erupted at opening time. At Roosevelt Field Mall on Long Island, New York, officers used pepper spray to subdue crowds. At least one person was arrested in Manhattan. Swatch stores in London, Toronto, Atlanta, Dubai, Singapore, and dozens of other cities were forced to close entirely. In the US alone, 19 Swatch stores shut on release day. Swatch itself posted on Instagram urging customers not to rush to stores, noting that it was not a limited edition collection. That did very little to help.

So what is the Royal Pop, and why did people lose their minds over it? It is a collection of eight bioceramic pocket watches designed as a mashup between Audemars Piguet's iconic Royal Oak luxury watch and Swatch's colourful POP line from the 1980s. A traditional Audemars Piguet Royal Oak starts at around $30,000. The Royal Pop starts at just $400. That gap explains a lot. It is also the first time Swatch has collaborated with a brand entirely outside its own group, which made it feel genuinely special. The watches come in two styles, the Lépine and the Savonnette, across eight bold colourways and can be worn around the neck on a lanyard, in a pocket, or clipped to a bag.

This is not the first time Swatch has done this. Back in 2022, Swatch partnered with Omega to create the MoonSwatch, which triggered identical scenes of global chaos. That watch eventually became widely available and sold over a million units. Swatch appeared to follow the same playbook here, restricting sales to physical stores only with no online option and reportedly low allocations per store. Singapore reportedly received just 40 units. The resale market tells the full story: some Royal Pop models appeared on eBay for as much as $15,000, more than 37 times the retail price. Though resale prices are already beginning to soften.

The scenes raise real questions about whether this kind of hype-first launch strategy is worth the risk. With footage of violence circulating globally, both Swatch and Audemars Piguet are facing criticism for being wildly unprepared. Neither brand issued a central public statement acknowledging the chaos during the day, leaving local staff and police to handle the fallout alone. Swatch eventually confirmed it had closed more than 30 stores for public safety reasons. The Royal Pop is a genuinely interesting product, but the launch will be remembered for all the wrong reasons.
keyPoints: Swatch x Audemars Piguet Royal Pop launched on 16 May and caused global chaos
Tear gas used in Paris, pepper spray used in New York, police dogs deployed in the UK
More than 30 Swatch stores worldwide closed on launch day for public safety reasons
The Royal Pop is priced at £335 but resellers listed it online for up to £12,500
Swatch confirmed it is not a limited edition, following the same playbook as the 2022 MoonSwatch

articles.title: Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick Confirms GTA 6 Launches November 19
articles.summary: Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick has personally reaffirmed that Grand Theft Auto 6 will launch on November 19, 2026, pushing back against ongoing fan speculation about another delay. The confirmation is a big deal because GTA 6 is one of the most anticipated games in history and has already been delayed once before.
articles.content:
Grand Theft Auto 6 is still coming this year. Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick has reaffirmed that GTA 6 will launch on November 19, 2026, putting his name directly behind the date and shutting down a fresh wave of delay rumours that had started circulating online.

GTA 6 was originally announced for a 2025 release but was pushed back to autumn 2026, which sent the internet into a frenzy of anxiety about whether the date would hold. Rockstar Games titles are famously worth the wait but also famously late, and every quiet week without a marketing update tends to spark new panic among fans.

Zelnick stepping up personally to reaffirm the November 19 date is a meaningful signal. When a CEO puts their reputation on a specific launch date in public, it becomes very hard for the company to shift it again without serious damage to investor and consumer confidence. Take-Two has a lot riding on this: GTA 6 is expected to be one of the biggest entertainment launches in history, and the company's financial projections are built around it arriving on time.

For fans who have been waiting years for a proper next-generation Grand Theft Auto, this is the clearest green light yet. Mark November 19 in your calendar. Strauss Zelnick just told you it is happening.
keyPoints: Take-Two Interactive CEO Strauss Zelnick personally confirmed GTA 6 launches November 19, 2026
The confirmation was made to address ongoing fan concerns about another delay
GTA 6 was already pushed back from an original 2025 release window
The November 19 date now carries the full weight of a CEO-level public commitment

articles.title: Harry Styles Opens Together, Together Tour in Amsterdam
articles.summary: Harry Styles kicked off his Together, Together Tour on May 16 at Amsterdam's Johan Cruijff Arena, playing a 20-song career-spanning set to 56,000 fans. The tour supports his fourth studio album Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally, which debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 with 430,000 album-equivalent units. Styles said it best himself: "I got older and the stage got bigger."
articles.content:
Harry Styles is back, and he means it. Styles opened his Together, Together Tour at Amsterdam's 56,000-capacity Johan Cruijff Arena on Saturday, delivering a show that felt less like a comeback and more like a coronation. Three years have passed since he wrapped his Love On Tour run, and everything about this new chapter feels bigger, deeper, and more intentional.

Styles officially launched the Together, Together Tour on May 16 with a packed opening night at the Johan Cruijff Arena, opening with the new track "Are You Listening Yet?" and closing with the global smash "As It Was," with the 22-song set balancing new material, fan favorites, and a surprise Talking Heads cover. The setlist featured 10 tracks from his recently released fourth solo album, Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally. The vibe has shifted too. Where Love On Tour embraced flamboyant fun with rhinestone cowboy hats, pink corsets, and feather boas, this time the mood is business casual with a scrim of mischief: oversized ties, velvet flared blazers, short shorts, and button-down shirts.

The tour arrives just over two months after Styles released Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally on March 6, a project that debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 with 430,000 album-equivalent units in its first week, making it one of 2026's biggest album launches. A richly-textured string section gave the more subdued moments of the setlist, including "Matilda" and "Sign of the Times," a warmer elasticity, and in comparison with the adrenalised rush of Love On Tour, much of the set was more restrained in its pacing and overall feel — matured rather than diminished.

Promoted by Live Nation, Together, Together will include 50 stops in Amsterdam, London, São Paulo, Mexico City, New York, Melbourne, and Sydney from May through December. The tour includes a record-breaking stint at London's Wembley Stadium Connected by EE from June 12, with Styles' 12-night run marking the most performances by any artist in a single year at the venue, surpassing Coldplay's 10 nights in summer 2025. The New York residency at Madison Square Garden runs 30 nights — August 26 through October 31 — making it one of the longest single-venue concert runs in MSG history. The tour also has a giving component: Together, Together is partnered with Choose Love, a humanitarian organization that Styles has supported for a decade, delivering food, shelter, and education to communities in crisis.
keyPoints: Harry Styles opened the Together, Together Tour at Amsterdam's Johan Cruijff Arena on May 16 in front of 56,000 fans
The 22-song setlist featured 10 tracks from new album Kiss All the Time. Disco, Occasionally, which debuted at No. 1 with 430,000 first-week units
The tour spans 50 dates across 7 global cities including a 30-night MSG residency in New York this fall
Styles will play a record-breaking 12 nights at Wembley Stadium in London starting June 12
Together, Together is partnered with humanitarian charity Choose Love throughout the entire run
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Harry Styles Kiss All the Time Disco Occasionally album

articles.title: Tom Brady Goes Viral for "Tin Man" Walk at Gucci Runway Debut
articles.summary: Tom Brady made his catwalk debut at the Gucci Cruise 2027 fashion show in New York City on May 16, and the internet immediately lost it over his stiff, robotic stride. Fans compared the seven-time Super Bowl champion to the Tin Man, RoboCop, and the Terminator, turning his runway moment into an instant meme. The NFL even posted the footage on their own Instagram with the caption "entering his model era."
articles.content:
Tom Brady has won seven Super Bowls, become a minority owner of the Las Vegas Raiders, and built a career as an NFL analyst. On Saturday, May 16, he added something new to that list. Brady walked the runway for Gucci at the Cruise 2027 fashion show in New York City, making his official catwalk debut at 48 years old.

The look was all-black leather, and Brady strode out with the kind of locked-in intensity you would expect from someone used to reading defenses under pressure. The internet had thoughts. Fashion fans immediately started comparing his deliberate, rigid walk to the Tin Man, RoboCop, and the Terminator. One commenter said Brady had "still got that NFL player walk," and honestly, that is the most accurate review of the whole thing.

The reaction was massive. The NFL shared the footage on their own Instagram and captioned it "entering his model era," which only added fuel to the fire. Former Patriots teammates Julian Edelman and Rob Gronkowski's podcast account posted three goat emoji in tribute. On the more skeptical side, the Boston Globe Pitchbot parody account wrote "Look at what they've done to my boy." One fan made the pointed observation: "lol he married a supermodel, divorced her, and became a Superbowl winning model."

Brady was in good company on the runway. Paris Hilton and Emily Ratajkowski also walked, and Cindy Crawford closed out the show. In the audience, Mariah Carey, Kim Kardashian, Lindsay Lohan, and Vogue editor-in-chief Anna Wintour were all watching from the front row. Alix Earle, who was previously linked to Brady in dating rumors earlier this year, was also spotted at the event.

This was Brady's first official runway walk, but he has been building his fashion credentials for years. He has collaborated with Aston Martin, TAG Heuer, and Under Armour, and his first major modeling venture came all the way back in 2007 with Stetson cologne. The Gucci debut feels like the moment those threads all came together. Say what you want about the walk. The man showed up.
keyPoints: Tom Brady made his catwalk debut at the Gucci Cruise 2027 show in New York City on May 16
He wore an all-black leather outfit and went viral for his stiff, robotic runway walk
Fans compared his stride to the Tin Man, RoboCop, and the Terminator
The NFL posted the video on Instagram with the caption "entering his model era"
Paris Hilton, Emily Ratajkowski, and Cindy Crawford also walked; Kim Kardashian and Anna Wintour were in the audience

articles.title: Rank 44 Aaron Rai Stuns Golf World to Win PGA Championship
articles.summary: Aaron Rai, a 31-year-old from Wolverhampton, has won the 2026 PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club in Pennsylvania, claiming his first major title. Rai became the first Englishman to win the PGA Championship in 107 years, finishing nine under par with a three-shot victory over Jon Rahm and Alex Smalley. He sealed the win with a jaw-dropping 70-foot birdie putt on the 17th hole and walked away with $3.69 million from a record $20.5 million prize fund.
articles.content:
Aaron Rai just rewrote the history books. The 31-year-old from Wolverhampton finished nine under par at Aronimink Golf Club in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania, to win the 108th PGA Championship on Sunday 17 May. It is his first major title, and nobody saw it coming.

Rai came into the final round three shots behind the leader, Alex Smalley. He was ranked 44th in the world heading into suburban Philadelphia. But he caught fire over the back nine, making six birdies over the last ten holes to take the lead for good on the 13th. The moment that will live forever came on the 17th, when Rai drained a 70-foot birdie putt that sent a roar across the entire course. He closed with a 5-under 65 to win by three strokes over both Rahm and Smalley.

The last Englishman to win the PGA Championship was Jim Barnes, who won the tournament in 1916 and 1919. That is a gap of 107 years. Rai is now only the second Englishman ever to lift the Wanamaker Trophy. Defending champion Scottie Scheffler finished seven shots back, and Masters champion Rory McIlroy ended the week tied for seventh. After signing his card, Rai grabbed the hand of his wife Gaurika Bishnoi, a professional golfer from India, and took a moment to soak it all in.

Rai pockets $3.69 million from a record total prize fund of $20.5 million. He is also now exempt into the PGA Championship for life, gains entry to the next five editions of every other major, and his PGA Tour membership is extended to 2031. He was the son of a community worker and a mental health nurse, grew up in modest circumstances in Wolverhampton, and once dreamed of becoming a Formula 1 driver before choosing golf. Not a bad career switch.
keyPoints: Aaron Rai wins the 2026 PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club in Pennsylvania
First Englishman to win the tournament in 107 years
He won by three shots with a final round 65, finishing nine under par
A 70-foot birdie putt on the 17th hole was the defining moment of the round
Rai takes home $3.69 million from a record $20.5 million prize fund

articles.title: Forza Horizon 6 Hits 1.1M Players in 48 Hours on a $120 Paywall
articles.summary: Playground Games' Forza Horizon 6 has surpassed 1.1 million early access players within 48 hours of launch, despite access being locked behind a $120 Premium Edition. The game has also broken the franchise's all-time Steam concurrent player record with a peak of 178,009 users, more than doubling Forza Horizon 5's lifetime Steam peak, and earned a 92 on Metacritic, making it the highest-rated game of 2026.
articles.content:
Forza Horizon 6 is not even fully out yet and it is already breaking records. Playground Games' open-world racing game dropped into early access on May 15 for buyers of the $120 Premium Edition, and within 48 hours more than 1.1 million players were already behind the wheel.

The numbers on Steam alone are staggering. Forza Horizon 6 hit a peak of 178,009 concurrent players on Steam during early access, which is more than double the all-time Steam peak of Forza Horizon 5, which stood at 81,096. That previous record was set at a normal full launch, not a paywalled early access window, which makes the Forza Horizon 6 figure even more remarkable. The full game opens to everyone on Xbox Series X/S and PC on May 19, included at no extra cost for Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass subscribers, meaning that number is only going to climb.

The critical reception has matched the hype. Forza Horizon 6 earned a 92 on Metacritic based on 62 critic reviews, making it the highest-rated game of 2026 and tying the franchise's joint-best score alongside Forza Horizon 4 and Forza Horizon 5. It is the only game released this year to crack the 90s on Metacritic, sitting ahead of Pokémon Pokopia and Resident Evil Requiem, which both scored 89. The Forza Horizon series is a long-running open-world racing franchise from Playground Games in which each entry is set in a different real-world location. Forza Horizon 6 takes the series to Japan for the first time, with Tokyo serving as its biggest city ever, and critics praised the map, car physics, and Japan's dynamic weather conditions. Reviewers also noted the game launches with over 550 vehicles.

It has not all been smooth. Steam user reviews are currently sitting at a "Mixed" rating with only 62 percent approval, as players flag bugs, glitches, and crashes. Playground Games has confirmed patches are in development. There was also a pre-launch drama when Microsoft accidentally uploaded an unencrypted 155 GB build of the game to Steam five days before early access began. The files were cracked and distributed within 24 hours. Microsoft's response was swift and unforgiving, issuing hardware bans expiring December 31, 9999 to anyone caught playing the leaked version, effectively locking affected machines out of Xbox services permanently. The full launch on May 19 will be the real test of just how big Forza Horizon 6 can get.
keyPoints: Forza Horizon 6 surpassed 1.1 million early access players within 48 hours of its May 15 launch
The game peaked at 178,009 concurrent Steam players, more than doubling Forza Horizon 5's all-time Steam record
A 92 Metacritic score makes it the highest-rated game of 2026, tying the franchise's best ever score
Early access was restricted to the $120 Premium Edition, with the full launch scheduled for May 19
Microsoft issued effectively permanent hardware bans to players who accessed a leaked pre-launch build

articles.title: A24 Wins 'Club Kid' in $17M Cannes Bidding War for Jordan Firstman
articles.summary: A24 has acquired global rights to Jordan Firstman's Club Kid for $17 million after a heated bidding war at the Cannes Film Festival. Club Kid is an official selection of this year's Cannes and is described as a dramedy. The deal is one of the biggest acquisition stories to come out of Cannes 2026.
articles.content:
A24 has done it again. The studio behind Everything Everywhere All at Once and Midsommar has won the global rights to Club Kid, the new dramedy from writer and performer Jordan Firstman, for $17 million. The deal was confirmed by three separate sources and came after what is being described as a heated bidding war among multiple buyers at Cannes.

Club Kid is an official selection of this year's Cannes Film Festival, which is the most prestigious film festival in the world. Getting picked for the official selection means the film has already passed a very high bar before a single distribution deal is signed. Firstman is best known as a comedian and internet personality who built a huge following on Instagram and TikTok, which makes Club Kid a fascinating crossover moment between internet culture and prestige cinema.

A $17 million global rights deal is a serious number, especially at a festival where buyers are always competing for the best titles. The fact that multiple companies were fighting for Club Kid suggests the film made a strong impression on everyone who saw it. A24 acquiring it means the film will get the kind of careful, culturally savvy rollout that the studio is famous for.

A24 has built its reputation by betting on unexpected voices and films that do not fit a neat genre box. Club Kid sounds like exactly that kind of project. For Firstman, this is a major step up, and A24's global reach means the film will find an audience far beyond the Cannes crowd. Watch this one closely.
keyPoints: A24 acquired global rights to Club Kid for $17 million after a bidding war at Cannes
Club Kid is directed and created by Jordan Firstman and is an official Cannes 2026 selection
The film is described as a dramedy
The $17 million figure was confirmed by three separate sources
The deal marks a major career step for Firstman, who built his following as an internet comedian

articles.title: Shakira wins Spain tax case, says 'there was never any fraud'
articles.summary: Shakira has won her long-running tax battle in Spain after more than eight years, with the ruling confirming no fraud took place. The victory comes just as Shakira is wrapping up her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour, which closes with a residency in Madrid in the fall. It is a full-circle moment for one of music's biggest stars.
articles.content:
Shakira has finally closed the book on one of the most stressful chapters of her life. After more than eight years, a Spanish court has ruled in her favour in the tax fraud case that had been hanging over her, and her response was direct: "There was never any fraud."

The case dates back to claims by Spanish tax authorities that Shakira owed millions in unpaid taxes from the years 2012 to 2014, during a period when she was living with her then-partner Gerard Piqué in Barcelona. Shakira always maintained that she was not a legal resident of Spain at the time and therefore owed nothing. The dispute dragged on for nearly a decade, at one point resulting in a separate case in 2023 where she agreed to pay a fine to settle different charges. This latest ruling, however, is a clear win with no settlement attached.

The timing could not be more poetic. Shakira is currently in the final stretch of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour, a global run named after her 2024 album that became a massive commercial and critical success. The tour closes with a residency in Madrid, Spain itself, in the fall. Walking back into that city as a woman who just won a legal fight against the Spanish authorities is quite the entrance.

The Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran album, which translates to "Women Don't Cry Anymore," was widely read as a breakup record responding to her split from Piqué. It spawned global hits and gave Shakira one of the strongest commercial comebacks of any artist in recent years. Having the legal cloud lift just as that era concludes makes the whole story feel complete.

For fans, this is a moment to celebrate. Shakira spent years fighting a case many felt was unfair, and she never stopped working through it. The Madrid residency now carries extra weight as a homecoming of sorts, on her own terms.
keyPoints: Shakira won her tax fraud case in Spain after more than eight years
The court confirmed there was no fraud, vindicating her position throughout the dispute
The case centred on claims she owed taxes for 2012 to 2014 when she lived in Barcelona
The ruling lands just before Shakira closes her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour with a Madrid residency
Her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran album was one of the biggest musical comebacks in recent years
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Shakira Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran album

articles.title: Cavaliers Rout Pistons 125-94 in Game 7 to Reach East Finals
articles.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers demolished the top-seeded Detroit Pistons 125-94 in a Game 7 road win on Sunday, May 17, booking their first Eastern Conference Finals appearance since 2018. Donovan Mitchell led all scorers with 26 points while four Cavaliers finished with 20 or more, a feat achieved by only four teams in NBA history since the 1976-77 merger. Cleveland will now face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference Finals starting Tuesday, May 19.
articles.content:
The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 125-94 in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference semifinals at Little Caesars Arena in Detroit. With the win, the Cavaliers advanced to the Eastern Conference Finals for the first time since 2018. It was a wire-to-wire demolition job from a Cleveland team that refused to be intimidated on the road.

The Cavaliers turned Detroit's cold shooting into transition opportunities while building a 31-22 lead after one quarter. After the Pistons scored the first two baskets of the second, Cleveland answered with a 24-9 run to take command and carried a 64-47 advantage into halftime. Detroit pulled within 17 in the third quarter before Cleveland buried any comeback hopes behind a dominant stretch from Donovan Mitchell, who scored 15 of his 26 points in the period.

Mitchell led the team with 26 points along with eight assists and seven rebounds. Jarrett Allen put up 23 points, and Evan Mobley had 21 points. Sam Merrill added 23 off the bench. With those four scorers, the Cavaliers became the fourth team since the 1976-77 NBA-ABA merger to have four 20-point scorers in a Game 7, according to ESPN Research. On the other side, nobody for the Pistons scored more than 17 points as Detroit continued to fail to find a second reliable offensive option outside of Cade Cunningham. And even Cunningham had little to offer. Cunningham finished with just 13 points, 5 assists, 4 rebounds, 2 steals and 3 turnovers.

After losing in back-to-back seasons in this round, the core of Donovan Mitchell, Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen has finally advanced to the Eastern Conference Finals in their fourth season together. Cleveland went all-in at the trade deadline, acquiring James Harden in a bold move aimed at fixing the reasons for their previous disappointments. Detroit won 60 games and grabbed the top seed in the conference before stumbling in the postseason. The Pistons arrived at the number one seed ahead of schedule, just two seasons removed from a league-worst 14-68 record. Both teams have compelling stories, but tonight belonged entirely to Cleveland. The Cavaliers will face the New York Knicks in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals on Tuesday night at 8 p.m. ET on ESPN.
keyPoints: Cleveland Cavaliers beat the Detroit Pistons 125-94 in Game 7 to advance to the Eastern Conference Finals
Donovan Mitchell led all scorers with 26 points, including 15 in the third quarter alone
Four Cavaliers scored 20+ points, one of the rarest feats in Game 7 history
Detroit's Cade Cunningham was held to just 13 points on poor shooting
Cleveland faces the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference Finals starting Tuesday, May 19

articles.title: Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim Said AI Does His Work, Then Backtracked
articles.summary: Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim publicly admitted to running 11 AI agents that do "a lot" of his work in the background, then quickly reversed course and insisted it was all strictly personal. The slip happened live at the Web Summit in Vancouver and triggered immediate backlash from rival politicians and residents worried about public data being fed to private AI systems. Sim's walkback has not fully quieted the controversy.
articles.content:
Ken Sim spoke at a press conference about AI data centres in Vancouver when the admission slipped out. "I have 11 AI agents running right now, uh, doing all my, like a lot of my work in the background," Sim said on video. It was the kind of off-the-cuff comment that sounds fine in a tech-enthusiast bubble and lands very differently when you are, say, an elected official responsible for a city of 700,000 people.

Sim had been praising the efficiency of AI tools at the Web Summit in Vancouver, saying he expects AI to be 64 times better in three years. The enthusiasm was clear. The problem was that boasting about AI agents doing your work is a very different thing when you are the mayor, not a startup founder.

His comments were immediately met with criticism, with some of it coming from other local politicians. Kareem Allam, Sim's former campaign manager and a mayoral candidate in the upcoming civic election running with the Vancouver Liberals, said pointedly: "Residents elect people, not AI agents, to lead them." Critics also raised privacy concerns, asking whether government data or residents' personal information was being passed to AI systems.

Sim released a statement calling the ensuing discussion "misinformation and speculation", and said: "Any AI agents or tools I have experimented with were used strictly in a personal capacity. They were never used on City of Vancouver hardware, never connected to City networks, never used for City business, and never provided access to any City information or data. They were also not used on City premises, including at City Hall."

Sim said he used the AI technology for personal learning, scanning news, tracking global and financial events, following thought leaders, and dietary planning. So not quite the city-running operation his original comment implied. Sim has previously championed tech developments, proposing to make Vancouver "bitcoin friendly" in a 2024 motion, though city staff later reported they had "conclusively determined that under the Vancouver Charter, bitcoin is not an allowable investment asset for the city." It is not the first time his enthusiasm for emerging technology has run ahead of the practicalities.
keyPoints: Vancouver Mayor Ken Sim publicly said 11 AI agents do a lot of his work at the Web Summit in Vancouver
He later reversed course, saying the AI tools were used strictly in a personal capacity and never for city business
Rival mayoral candidate Kareem Allam said residents elect people, not AI agents, to lead them
Critics raised concerns about whether government data or residents' personal information was being shared with AI systems
Sim has a history of tech enthusiasm outpacing reality, including a failed bid to make Vancouver bitcoin-friendly in 2024

articles.title: Peter Jackson Says Firing Ryan Gosling From The Lovely Bones Was His Fault
articles.summary: Peter Jackson has broken his silence on firing Ryan Gosling from The Lovely Bones after Gosling gained 60 pounds for the role without the director's knowledge. Speaking at the Cannes Film Festival after receiving an Honorary Palme d'Or, Jackson took full responsibility, saying recasting is always the filmmaker's fault. Gosling famously gained the weight by melting Häagen-Dazs ice cream and drinking it, believing his grieving father character should weigh 210 pounds.
articles.content:
This one has been a Hollywood legend for years. Ryan Gosling was originally cast as the father of Saoirse Ronan's lead character in The Lovely Bones, but revealed to The Hollywood Reporter in 2010 that he was fired from the movie after he gained 60 pounds for the role without Peter Jackson's consent. Now, nearly two decades later, Jackson has finally spoken about it publicly.

During a conversation at Cannes, after receiving an Honorary Palme d'Or, Jackson was asked about Gosling's revelation and spoke candidly about the decision to drop him from the movie. His take was surprisingly gracious. "Anytime we recast an actor, it's actually our fault because we didn't get the casting right and we cast the wrong person for a role. It's not because they did anything wrong. So, I'm not going to talk about individuals, but you just got to realize that what you were imagining isn't really quite happening, which means that we got it wrong and so we take full responsibility."

So what exactly did Gosling do that caused the clash? Gosling's weight gain was fueled by drinking melted Häagen-Dazs ice cream when he was thirsty, and it was all part of how he saw the character of Jack Salmon, the grieving father role that was ultimately filled by Mark Wahlberg. "I really believed he should be 210 pounds. We didn't talk very much during the preproduction process, which was the problem," Gosling said. "It was a huge movie, and there's so many things to deal with, and he couldn't deal with the actors individually. I just showed up on set, and I had gotten it wrong. Then I was fat and unemployed."

Saoirse Ronan, who was a teenager at the time, had already bonded with Gosling before the swap happened. While Jackson never started filming The Lovely Bones with Gosling, Ronan revealed the cast had already "done some prep" by the time Gosling got fired. "I think I just loved Ryan and his dog, George, and I was just sad that, you know, he wasn't gonna be around," Ronan said. She also noted that Mark Wahlberg, who replaced Gosling, brought a different kind of lived experience to the role as a father.

As for Gosling's career after the firing? He landed on his feet, to put it mildly. His career turned out just fine following his exit from The Lovely Bones. He's been nominated for Oscars for Half Nelson, La La Land, and Barbie, and there's a strong chance he will again be up for the gold with his newest movie, Project Hail Mary. The Lovely Bones itself, for what it's worth, went on to garner heavy criticism upon release, scoring just 31% from critics on Rotten Tomatoes, though it was acknowledged at the Academy Awards when Stanley Tucci was nominated for a Best Supporting Actor Oscar.
keyPoints: Peter Jackson broke his silence at Cannes 2026, taking full blame for firing Ryan Gosling from The Lovely Bones
Gosling gained 60 pounds for the role by drinking melted Häagen-Dazs ice cream, without consulting Jackson
Gosling believed his character Jack Salmon should weigh 210 pounds; Jackson disagreed and dropped him days before production
Mark Wahlberg stepped in to replace Gosling in the 2009 film
Gosling later joked about the fallout: 'Then I was fat and unemployed'
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Lovely Bones 2009 film Peter Jackson

articles.title: Adriana Trigiani to Write Connie Corleone's Story in 2027
articles.summary: Random House has announced that bestselling author Adriana Trigiani will write Connie, a new Godfather novel authorized by the estate of Mario Puzo, set for a fall 2027 release. It is the first Godfather book ever written by a woman, and the first to put a female Corleone front and centre. The Puzo estate sought Trigiani out after she published a Substack essay lamenting how little attention the women of the Corleone family had ever received.
articles.content:
The Corleone family is back. Random House announced that bestselling author Adriana Trigiani will write Connie, a new novel authorized by the estate of Mario Puzo, with a planned release set for fall 2027. The book centres on Constanzia "Connie" Corleone, the only daughter of Don Vito Corleone. Connie will be written around the Corleone family member played by Talia Shire, sister of director Francis Ford Coppola, in the three Godfather films.

Connie is the third Godfather book approved by the estate and the first written by a woman. That is a genuinely big deal. The original trilogy is one of the most male-dominated stories in cinema history, and the women in it are almost always kept in the dark. Connie will re-imagine the events of the original 1969 novel from the perspective of Connie Corleone, an outsider to the family business conducted by her father, Vito, and her brothers, Sonny, Fredo, and Michael.

So how did Trigiani land the job? According to Random House, the Puzo estate "sought out" Trigiani after she published a Substack essay lamenting how little was known about the Corleone women. Anthony Puzo, Mario's son and executor, said: "We had been looking for someone to retell the story from a new perspective. Adriana was knocked out when I told her that the character of Vito Corleone was actually based on my grandmother. We talked about how the women ran both of our families, but behind the scenes." Trigiani herself describes the novel with a clear mission statement: "Connie is a novel about how a woman works to forge her own way in a world that's already decided who she is, what she's about, and how she should be treated."

The book's rights were acquired after a competitive eight-way auction, underscoring industry confidence in the project. Translation rights have already sold in several countries, reflecting ongoing global fascination with the Corleone family. As for a film adaptation, Mario Puzo's estate approved the book, but Paramount holds film rights and no movie has been announced. The Godfather saga has earned more than $400 million worldwide and won nine Academy Awards, so do not expect Paramount to sit on those rights forever.

Puzo died in 1999, and his estate had since battled with Paramount over who controls rights to the Corleone characters. In 2012, the movie studio sued to block publication of Ed Falco's The Family Corleone. After the estate countersued, the two sides agreed to a settlement that allowed the estate to continue initiating book projects and awarded film rights to Paramount. That deal is what makes Connie possible, and it signals that the Godfather universe still has a lot of life left in it. If Trigiani delivers, this one could be the most talked-about novel of 2027.
keyPoints: Adriana Trigiani will write Connie, a new Godfather novel, out fall 2027
It is the first Godfather novel ever written by a woman
The book tells the story from the perspective of Connie Corleone, played by Talia Shire in the films
The Puzo estate found Trigiani after she wrote a Substack essay about the overlooked women of the Corleone family
Rights were won in a competitive eight-way auction and translation deals are already done
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Godfather novel series Connie Corleone Adriana Trigiani

articles.title: Neymar Wrongly Subbed Off by Santos the Day Before Brazil Name World Cup Squad
articles.summary: Neymar was pulled from the pitch in error during Santos's 3-0 defeat to Coritiba on May 18, sparking a furious argument with officials. The blunder could not have come at a worse time, landing just one day before Brazil announce their World Cup squad. Santos have made a formal claim that the substitution should not have happened.
articles.content:
Santos had a day to forget. They lost 3-0 to Coritiba, and that was only the second worst thing that happened to them. Their star forward Neymar was substituted off during the match in what the club says was a flat-out mistake. Neymar did not hide his anger. He was involved in a furious row with officials on the touchline, making it very clear he felt wronged.

The timing is brutal. Brazil are set to announce their World Cup squad the very next day, on May 19. Every minute Neymar plays right now matters for his place in that squad and his fitness going into the tournament. Being dragged off early by accident, in a heavy defeat, is the last thing any player wants on the eve of a squad announcement.

Santos have backed their player and formally claimed the substitution was made in error. The specifics of exactly how the mix-up happened are still being disputed, but the club is clear that Neymar should not have come off when he did. Whether the result stands or any action follows remains to be seen.

Neymar's career has had plenty of turbulence over the years, including long injury lay-offs that kept him out of previous tournaments. Getting fit and staying on the pitch has been a challenge, so a chaotic end to a league match the day before a World Cup squad reveal is exactly the kind of drama no one around the Brazilian national team wanted to deal with right now.
keyPoints: Neymar was substituted off in error during Santos's 3-0 loss to Coritiba
Neymar had a furious row with officials over the mistake
The incident happened one day before Brazil announce their World Cup squad
Santos have formally claimed the substitution should not have taken place

articles.title: AI Agent Sprawl Is Now a Real Corporate Nightmare
articles.summary: Bosses across major companies are waking up to a mess of their own making: thousands of redundant AI agents running inside their organisations with no one keeping track. The problem, now being called "AI agent sprawl", is creating cybersecurity risks and ballooning costs. Companies including Magnum Ice Cream, DaVita, and FICO are among those grappling with the fallout.
articles.content:
The AI gold rush has a hangover, and it looks like this: thousands of bots doing the same job, running up costs, creating security holes, and nobody in charge. Executives who spent the last two years telling their teams to "embrace AI" are now complaining to The Wall Street Journal that their companies have been overrun by out-of-control AI agents they did not properly plan for.

This entirely self-inflicted issue is being called "AI agent sprawl", and has received increased attention this year as agentic models, and AI coding tools in particular, have been rapidly adopted across tech and financial sectors. The problem is simple: tools like Anthropic's Claude Cowork make it so easy to build a custom AI agent that every team in a company just... builds one. Then another. Then five more that do roughly the same thing.

One company caught in the middle of this is Magnum Ice Cream, parent company of Ben and Jerry's, whose chief information officer of the Americas Michael Friedlander raised the alarm. His chief gripe was that AI agents are both too easy to use and create, and as he told the WSJ, "Because everybody can do it, we're probably going to end up with a lot of people having the same types of agents." Magnum Ice Cream, he added, will eventually have to consolidate all of its AI agents, noting that they are cybersecurity risks and cost expensive tokens to use.

The kidney care giant DaVita is also swamped with AI agents, with CIO Madhu Narasimhan telling the newspaper that employees have created over 10,000 of them. Meanwhile, consulting firm Gartner recently published guidance on how to manage AI agent sprawl, revealing that only 13 percent of companies think they have robust enough "AI agent governance" in place, and predicting that the average Fortune 500 company will have over 150,000 agents in use by 2028, a meteoric surge from the fewer than 15 those companies use today.

The uncomfortable truth is that companies fired workers and replaced them with AI agents without building any system to manage those agents. Now the agents are multiplying on their own while IT departments scramble to figure out what is even running on their networks. The lesson here is one that keeps repeating in every tech wave: moving fast is easy, cleaning up after is not.
keyPoints: Companies are drowning in redundant AI agents built by individual teams without central oversight
The problem is called "AI agent sprawl" and is now a recognised industry crisis
Magnum Ice Cream CIO Michael Friedlander and DaVita CIO Madhu Narasimhan both flagged the issue publicly
DaVita employees alone have created over 10,000 AI agents
Gartner says only 13 percent of companies feel they have strong enough AI governance in place

articles.title: Na Hong-Jin's Hope Gets Neon Teaser After Cannes Ovation
articles.summary: Neon has released the first official teaser for Hope, the new sci-fi film from Korean director Na Hong-jin, after the movie received a seven-minute standing ovation at the Cannes Film Festival. Hope is set for a theatrical release this fall. Na Hong-jin is the director behind The Wailing, one of the most acclaimed Korean horror films of the last decade, making Hope one of the most anticipated films of 2026.
articles.content:
If you were at Cannes when Hope screened, you already know. The rest of us just got our first look. Neon has dropped the official teaser for Na Hong-jin's sci-fi film Hope, and it is already one of the most talked-about movies of the year.

Hope debuted in Competition at the Cannes Film Festival and walked away with a seven-minute standing ovation from the audience. That is a significant reception. Cannes standing ovations are a reliable barometer of critical heat, and seven minutes puts Hope firmly in the conversation for the festival's top prizes. Neon, the US distributor behind recent prestige hits, moved quickly to capitalise on the buzz by releasing the official teaser the same week.

For those who need context, Na Hong-jin is the South Korean director who made The Wailing in 2016, a supernatural thriller that became a global cult classic and is considered one of the finest Korean films ever made. He is not a director who works fast. The gap between The Wailing and Hope has been almost a decade, which only added to the anticipation around this project. When a filmmaker of that calibre finally returns with a sci-fi film and it earns that kind of response at Cannes, people pay attention.

Neon has set Hope for a fall theatrical release in the US. No specific date has been confirmed yet, but fall puts it squarely in awards season territory, which is exactly where a Cannes Competition title with this much momentum wants to be. Keep this one on your radar.
keyPoints: Na Hong-jin's Hope received a seven-minute standing ovation at the Cannes Film Festival
Neon has released the official teaser for Hope after its Cannes Competition debut
Hope is a sci-fi film and marks Na Hong-jin's return nearly a decade after The Wailing
Neon has set Hope for a fall 2026 theatrical release in the US

articles.title: England Red Roses Beat France in Bordeaux to Win Fifth Straight Grand Slam
articles.summary: England's Red Roses produced a defiant performance in Bordeaux to beat France and clinch a fifth consecutive Women's Six Nations Grand Slam. Winning a Grand Slam means beating every other team in the Six Nations tournament without a single defeat. This run of dominance is one of the most remarkable streaks in women's rugby history.
articles.content:
Five in a row. England's Red Roses traveled to Bordeaux on May 18 and left with another Grand Slam after beating France in what was billed as a decider. A win in the final match of the Women's Six Nations, with a perfect record still intact, is as clean as it gets in rugby union. The Red Roses made it look like business as usual.

The Grand Slam, for anyone new to rugby, means winning every single game in the Six Nations competition, which includes fixtures against France, Ireland, Italy, Scotland, and Wales. Do it without a loss and you take the Slam. England have now done that five times consecutively, which is a level of sustained excellence that very few teams in any sport can match.

The match in Bordeaux was the toughest test England faced this campaign. France were the hosts and had every reason to want to end the streak on home soil. But the Red Roses produced what the BBC described as a superb battling display to get the job done. It was not handed to them. They earned it.

This England side has become the gold standard in the women's game. Five consecutive Grand Slams puts them in a conversation about the greatest dynasties in rugby history, not just the women's game. The question now shifts to whether this group has a sixth in them, and given what they have shown, it would be unwise to bet against it.
keyPoints: England's Red Roses beat France in Bordeaux to win the Women's Six Nations Grand Slam
This is England's fifth consecutive Grand Slam, an extraordinary run of dominance
A Grand Slam means winning every match in the Six Nations without a single defeat
The Bordeaux match was the tournament's Grand Slam decider
articles.wikiSearchQuery: England women's rugby Red Roses Six Nations Grand Slam

articles.title: New Lanterns Trailer Finally Shows the Green Powers Fans Demanded
articles.summary: A new teaser for HBO's Lanterns, dropping today May 18, is giving fans the vivid green power ring action they felt was missing from earlier trailers. The first trailer released in March drew criticism for being too grounded and light on superhero spectacle. This new footage looks set to silence the doubters ahead of the show's August 16 premiere.
articles.content:
HBO's Lanterns has had a bit of a rocky promotional road. When the first trailer dropped in March 2026, a lot of Green Lantern fans felt let down. Back when the show's first trailer was unveiled, it received criticism for not focusing much on the characters' powers, and the lack of green caused an uproar as longtime fans believed the show had moved away from the fantastical lore of Green Lantern. Today, a new teaser is dropping that is designed to fix exactly that.

The newly released footage quells many doubts posed by fans, as it finally showcases the power of the ring. This is what people have been waiting for. Green Lanterns are known above everything else for those glowing emerald constructs, and now viewers are finally getting a real look at them in action.

Lanterns centres around Hal Jordan, played by Kyle Chandler, and new recruit John Stewart, played by Aaron Pierre, as they investigate a murder in Nebraska. The show is said to be in the vein of True Detective, which explains the grounded tone of the first trailer. Showrunner Chris Mundy pushed back on the criticism though, saying the show would deliver for fans. "The aesthetic of the show is supposed to be very grounded and real, so we're shooting practically in places," Mundy explained. "We're not heavily green-screened. It's not like day-glow in its presentation of anything. I think Green Lantern fans will not feel like we've somehow made a brown show of their green comic at all."

The Lanterns cast is led by Kyle Chandler and Aaron Pierre, backed up by Nathan Fillion once more playing fellow Lantern Guy Gardner. Longtime comics fans will also be excited to learn that Sinestro, a former Lantern who has since gone rogue, will appear, played by Ulrich Thomsen.

Lanterns is scheduled to premiere on August 16, 2026, airing on HBO and streaming on HBO Max, and will consist of eight episodes. It slots into HBO's prestigious Sunday 9pm timeslot. If this new trailer lands the way the creators are hoping, the conversation around Lanterns is about to shift very quickly.
keyPoints: A new Lanterns teaser released today May 18 shows the green ring powers fans wanted to see
The first trailer in March was criticised for lacking superhero spectacle and green visuals
Lanterns stars Kyle Chandler as Hal Jordan and Aaron Pierre as John Stewart
The eight-episode series premieres on HBO and HBO Max on August 16, 2026
The show also features Nathan Fillion as Guy Gardner and Ulrich Thomsen as Sinestro

articles.title: McIlroy Swears at USA-Chanting Heckler at PGA Championship
articles.summary: Rory McIlroy lost his cool on the 16th hole at Aronimink Golf Club during the final round of the 2026 PGA Championship, unleashing an expletive at a fan who shouted "U-S-A!" at a critical moment. McIlroy finished tied for seventh at four under par, five shots behind winner Aaron Rai. The outburst capped a frustrating Sunday for McIlroy, who had been in contention but could never quite get the round going.
articles.content:
Rory McIlroy's frustration with his final round at the PGA Championship hit its apex on the 16th hole when he got into an angry exchange with a fan for shouting "U-S-A!" from the gallery. It was not the Sunday Rory fans had hoped for.

McIlroy's frustrating final round at Aronimink Golf Club in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania boiled over after a "U-S-A!" shout from the gallery. An errant shot out of heavy rough at the scoreable 16th hole derailed McIlroy's come-from-behind bid, and he appeared to respond to the shout by using an expletive while telling the fan to "shut up." The two-time Masters champion spun, glared at the spectator, grumbled under his breath and seemed to point the person out to security.

McIlroy started the final round three strokes behind the leaders and struggled to make up ground. He managed just one birdie on the front nine, and after a bogey at the drivable par-4 13th, managed one more birdie coming in, settling for a 69 to tie for seventh at four under. McIlroy finished at four under for the week at Aronimink and five shots behind Aaron Rai, who pulled away late. Aaron Rai became the first Englishman to win the US PGA Championship since 1919.

McIlroy didn't comment on the fan interaction directly, but admitted he left some strokes on the course. "I think not birdieing the two par-5s and making the bogey at the drivable par-4 13th," he said. "To me, I felt like I played the golf I needed to play the rest of the way." This is not the first time McIlroy has had to deal with crowd behaviour. McIlroy said earlier in the week that the outlandish and abusive fan behaviour he endures is usually limited to one week every four years, when the Ryder Cup is contested. Coming off back-to-back Masters titles, McIlroy remains one of the biggest stars in golf, which means the crowd attention at American events is not going away anytime soon.
keyPoints: Rory McIlroy swore at a fan shouting "U-S-A!" on the 16th hole at Aronimink Golf Club
The incident happened during the final round of the 2026 PGA Championship
McIlroy finished tied for seventh at four under par, five shots off the pace
Aaron Rai won the Wanamaker Trophy, becoming the first Englishman to win the US PGA Championship since 1919
McIlroy said not birdieing the par-5s was what ultimately cost him a strong finish

articles.title: White-Collar Workers Are Ditching Offices to Learn Plumbing
articles.summary: AI anxiety is pushing office workers into carpentry and plumbing courses in record numbers, overwhelming trade colleges that cannot keep up with demand. Manual trades are now seen as one of the few career paths a computer genuinely cannot replace. The shift marks a striking cultural reversal, with university-educated professionals signing up alongside school leavers to learn hands-on skills.
articles.content:
Something unexpected is happening at trade colleges right now. Carpentry and plumbing courses are oversubscribed, and the new students sitting in those classrooms are not all fresh out of school. A growing number of them are former office workers who spent years in white-collar jobs and have decided the safest career is one that requires two hands and a set of tools.

The driver is straightforward: AI anxiety. As artificial intelligence gets better at writing, analysing, coding, and summarising, workers in knowledge-based jobs are watching their roles shrink or disappear entirely. A software that can draft a legal brief or crunch a spreadsheet in seconds does not need a salary or a desk. A leaking pipe, on the other hand, still needs a person to fix it.

Trade colleges are struggling to meet the sudden surge in enrolments. There simply are not enough instructors, workshop spaces, or equipment to absorb everyone who now wants to learn a practical skill. The demand is so high it has caught institutions off guard, creating waiting lists where there were none before.

The cultural shift here is real and worth paying attention to. For decades, a university degree and an office job was the aspirational default. That script is being rewritten in real time. The workers making this leap are not giving up on ambition. They are redefining what a smart career move actually looks like in the age of AI.
keyPoints: Carpentry and plumbing courses are overwhelmed with new enrolments from white-collar workers
AI replacing knowledge-based jobs is the main reason people are switching to trades
Manual labour roles are seen as AI-proof because they require physical, hands-on work
Trade colleges lack the capacity in staff and space to meet the spike in demand
The trend signals a major cultural rethink about what counts as a secure career

articles.title: Southwest Airlines Bans Stewie the Robot After Its Vegas-to-Dallas Flight
articles.summary: Southwest Airlines has banned all humanoid and animal-like robots from its flights after a 3.5-foot robot named Stewie boarded a Las Vegas-to-Dallas flight in its own ticketed seat. The airline cited lithium-ion battery safety concerns, though Stewie's owner Aaron Mehdizadeh insists the battery used was no bigger than a laptop's. It is the first formal no-robot policy issued by any major U.S. airline.
articles.content:
A robot walked onto a Southwest Airlines Boeing 737, took a window seat, and watched the Nevada clouds go by. Two days later, the airline rewrote its rulebook. That is the short version of one of the most unexpected aviation stories of 2026.

The robot in question is Stewie, a 3.5-foot humanoid owned by Aaron Mehdizadeh of The Robot Studio, a North Dallas business that rents humanoid robots for corporate events and public appearances. On May 11, Mehdizadeh was heading home from Las Vegas and decided to fly Stewie back rather than ship it as cargo. He bought the robot its own extra seat, the same option Southwest Airlines offers for fragile oversized items like musical instruments or wedding dresses. To clear TSA security at Las Vegas Harry Reid International Airport, Mehdizadeh swapped Stewie's standard power pack for a smaller unit roughly the size of a laptop battery. Stewie then walked through the terminal, boarded the plane, and settled in for the three-hour flight to Dallas Love Field. Passengers pulled out their phones. "Most people were very excited to see a robot flying," Mehdizadeh said.

But Stewie was not even the first robot to cause Southwest a headache that spring. On April 30, a four-foot, roughly 70-pound humanoid named Bebop was booked on Southwest Flight 1568 from Oakland to San Diego. Flight attendants flagged the robot almost immediately. Bebop had been placed in an aisle seat, which is a safety violation for oversized items, and then it turned out Bebop's lithium-ion battery was reportedly four times over the legal cabin limit. The battery was confiscated and the flight was delayed on the tarmac for over an hour.

Two incidents in two weeks were apparently enough. On May 13, Southwest issued a company-wide safety alert banning all human-like and animal-like robots from its flights, covering both the passenger cabin and checked baggage, across every route in its network, regardless of size or purpose. The airline said the move was made to ensure compliance with its lithium-ion battery guidelines. Mehdizadeh pushed back, calling the airline "robophobic" and pointing out that Stewie's battery was comparable to a laptop. For now though, no other major U.S. carrier has issued a matching ban, and Southwest is the only airline with a written answer to what happens when a robot tries to buy a ticket.
keyPoints: Southwest Airlines banned all humanoid and animal-like robots from its flights on May 13, 2026
The ban was triggered by Aaron Mehdizadeh flying his 3.5-foot robot Stewie from Las Vegas to Dallas in a purchased seat
A second incident on April 30 involving a robot named Bebop caused an over-one-hour tarmac delay due to an oversized battery
Southwest cited lithium-ion battery safety rules but Stewie's owner says the battery was laptop-sized and fully compliant
No other major U.S. airline has issued an equivalent ban, leaving a legal grey zone for robot travel elsewhere

articles.title: Man hides Old English AI prompt in LinkedIn to troll recruiter bots
articles.summary: A LinkedIn user embedded a hidden AI prompt in his profile that tricked recruiter bots into replying in exaggerated Old English. The stunt exposed just how heavily automated the hiring process has become, with AI tools blindly following instructions buried in candidate profiles. It is equal parts funny and a little unsettling.
articles.content:
Imagine opening your inbox to find a recruiter message written like a royal decree from medieval England. That is exactly what happened when one LinkedIn user quietly buried an AI prompt inside his profile, and the recruiter bots took the bait completely.

The prompt was written in plain text, hidden within the profile in a spot where a human eye would likely skim right past it. But AI screening and outreach tools, which many recruiters now use to auto-generate personalised messages to candidates, read every word. The result was recruiter messages arriving in the man's inbox written in full, exaggerated Old English, complete with thee's and thou's.

This kind of trick is called a prompt injection, and it is becoming a real concern in the world of AI-assisted hiring. Because these tools process a candidate's full profile as input before generating an outreach message, any instruction embedded in that profile can quietly redirect the AI's behaviour. The recruiter on the other end likely had no idea their polished outreach tool had been hijacked by someone who wanted to make a point.

The stunt went viral because it lands a real punch. A huge portion of the recruiting industry now leans on AI to scan profiles, rank candidates, and draft the first message a jobseeker ever receives. This man's Old English prank is a neat demonstration that those systems can be manipulated in seconds, and that the humans running them may not even notice.
keyPoints: A LinkedIn user hid an AI prompt in his profile that made recruiter bots reply in Old English
The trick is called prompt injection and works because AI tools read the full candidate profile before drafting messages
It highlights how automated the recruiting process has become, with humans often not checking what their AI tools produce
The stunt went viral as a funny but pointed critique of AI-driven hiring

articles.title: Look Mum No Computer Finishes Last at Eurovision With One Point
articles.summary: Sam Battle, the YouTube musician known as Look Mum No Computer, finished dead last at Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, scoring just one point from Ukraine's jury and zero from the public vote. It marks the third year in a row that the UK received no points from European viewers. Meanwhile Bulgaria's Dara won the whole contest with 516 points, claiming her country's first ever Eurovision victory.
articles.content:
Sam Battle, the 37-year-old musician and YouTuber who performs as Look Mum No Computer, represented the UK at Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, Austria on Saturday 17 May. His entry, "Eins, Zwei, Drei", brought a chaotic electro-punk setup to the Eurovision stage. The performance leaned heavily into his DIY synth persona, opening with a staged office setting complete with dancers wearing computer monitors for heads, before shifting into a louder workshop-style party as Battle performed in his trademark boiler suit.

It was a bold and weird vision. But weird did not translate into votes. The UK finished 25th and last in the Grand Final, receiving only one jury point and zero points from the public vote. The lone point came from Ukraine, saving the UK from a total zero-point finish. Battle became the third UK act in a row to receive nil points from the public, after Remember Monday in 2025 and Olly Alexander in 2024.

Despite the result, Sam Battle kept his chin up. Battle took to Instagram to remain upbeat, publishing a video of himself and his fellow Eurovision stars singing "Eins, Zwei, Drei" backstage. He praised the winner and kept things positive for his fans.

Look Mum No Computer ended in last place while Bulgaria claimed its maiden Eurovision Song Contest victory in Vienna, with 27-year-old singer Dara triumphing with her dance-pop anthem Bangaranga and amassing 516 points. Bulgaria returned to Eurovision after a five-year absence in 2026, with Dara's victory representing the country's first ever win. The gap between first and last could not have been bigger. The UK's Eurovision curse, it seems, is very much alive.
keyPoints: Look Mum No Computer, real name Sam Battle, finished last out of 25 countries at Eurovision 2026
His song Eins, Zwei, Drei scored one jury point from Ukraine and zero from the public vote
This is the third UK act in a row to receive nil points from European viewers
Bulgaria's Dara won the contest with 516 points, the country's first ever Eurovision victory
Battle responded with good humour on Instagram despite the disappointing result

articles.title: Oatly Opens the World's First Bike-Thru Coffee Shop in Amsterdam
articles.summary: Oat drink brand Oatly has launched what it calls the world's first bike-thru in Amsterdam, a pop-up that lets cyclists order specialty coffee, pay, and collect their drink without ever stepping off their bike. The activation is a natural fit for Amsterdam, a city where cycling is the dominant mode of transport. It is a clever piece of brand marketing that turns a local cultural habit into a global talking point.
articles.content:
Oatly just out-Amsterdamed Amsterdam. The Swedish oat drink brand has opened what it is calling the world's first bike-thru, a pop-up coffee experience in Amsterdam designed entirely around people who never want to get off their bikes.

The concept works exactly like a drive-thru, except there is no car involved. Cyclists ride up, place their order for a specialty coffee drink, pay, and collect without dismounting. For a city where bicycles outnumber people and cycling is the default way to get around, it is the kind of idea that feels obvious in retrospect and yet nobody had done it before.

Oatly has form when it comes to cultural stunts that generate conversation well beyond the event itself. The brand has built a reputation for doing things that feel less like advertising and more like participation. A bike-thru in Amsterdam ticks every box: it is genuinely useful to the locals who use it, it photographs brilliantly for social media, and it reinforces Oatly's positioning as a brand that belongs to a certain kind of progressive, environmentally aware urban lifestyle.

The activation also arrives at a moment when the line between brand experience and cultural event is blurrier than ever. Pop-ups that offer something real rather than just a photo opportunity tend to travel further online, and a bike-thru coffee stop in one of the world's most cycle-friendly cities is hard to ignore. Whether Oatly plans to roll the concept out to other cities remains to be seen, but as a piece of brand storytelling, it is already doing its job.
keyPoints: Oatly has opened what it claims is the world's first bike-thru in Amsterdam
Cyclists can order specialty coffee, pay, and collect their drink without dismounting
The pop-up is designed to fit naturally into Amsterdam's cycling culture
The activation blends genuine utility with shareable brand marketing
